chapter_title.en: Chapter on the Ruling for One Whose Circumambulation is Interrupted by Prayer or Other Matters

text: : قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ اَلرَّجُلُ يَأْتِي أَخَاهُ وَ هُوَ فِي اَلطَّوَافِ فَقَالَ «يَخْرُجُ مَعَهُ فِي حَاجَتِهِ ثُمَّ يَرْجِعُ وَ يَبْنِي عَلَى طَوَافِهِ».

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.2799 - It is narrated from Safwan al-Jammal, who said: I said to Abu Abdullah (as), "What about a man who meets his brother while performing tawaf?"
Imam (as) said: "He may leave with him to fulfill his need, then return and continue his tawaf from where he left off."
